name: Calculus
description: Differential calculus, integral calculus, ordinary differential equations. The mathematics of rates of change, accumulation, and continuous-time dynamics. Foundation of all physics modeling, control theory, and economic dynamics. Depends on 444 (algebra) for limit structure and field properties. Feeds into 533 (analysis as its rigorous foundation), 555 (topology via differentiable manifolds), and 566 (linear algebra via Jacobians and gradients).
axioms: Fundamental theorem of calculus (Parts I & II): differentiation and integration are inverse operations
Mean value theorem: ∃ c ∈ (a,b) such that f'(c) = (f(b)-f(a))/(b-a)
Leibniz integral rule: differentiation under the integral sign
Existence and uniqueness theorem for ODE initial value problems (Picard-Lindelöf)
Taylor's theorem: Cⁿ functions can be approximated by polynomials
reasoning_patterns: ODE classification (linear/nonlinear, autonomous, order) and solving
boundary_conditions: Not all functions are differentiable (Weierstrass function is continuous but nowhere differentiable)
Not all ODEs have closed-form solutions — most real systems require numerical methods
Many integrals have no elementary antiderivative (Liouville's theorem)
Taylor series may converge to wrong function (smooth non-analytic functions exist)
